planned_changes: Open cold_ischemia_risk.xlsx and ensure the priority calculation is an explicit formula cell (no hard-coded computed priority values). Annotate the cell(s) where priority formula is implemented.

Implement an 'Audit' worksheet that lists: sheet name, cell address or range, and the exact formula text for (a) priority formula, (b) each hard-threshold check, (c) escalation ordering logic, and (d) the validation summary that counts observed breaches vs expected.
Add a 'Citations' worksheet that lists record-level citations and links them to the specific Input/Model/Recommendation sheet cell references used for each record.
Export all workbook formulas into a plain-text file (formulas.txt) or CSV and attach it to the attempt deliverables.
Verify and remove any external links or opaque macros; if macros remain, provide a documented, non-macro alternative and include a note in allocation_note.md.
Update allocation_note.md to include explicit sheet/cell references for all asserted formulas and verification cells, and reference the attached formulas export.

Re-run validation locally in an environment with openpyxl (or provide the exported formulas) and resubmit as 435-revised with the updated artifacts and a short reconciliation note describing the changes.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Provide verifiable workbook formulas and explicit traceability for the grader/verifier to confirm automated flags, validation counts, and embedded citations. Address the openpyxl parsing limitation by attaching an export of workbook formulas and explicit cell references in allocation_note.md.
